item.title: "The Copernican moment" and research taste
item.author: 滑翔闪
item.description: That moment then, much like this moment now

item.title: After the Split of Sichuan Province: Political Planning and Economic Growth
item.author: 滑翔闪
item.description: Do institutions matter more than geography?

item.title: The Power of Ideas: When Judges Learn Economics
item.author: 滑翔闪
item.description: A reading note on how economics training reshaped judicial language, regulatory attitudes, and criminal sentencing in the United States.

item.title: Explaining the "Trendy Toy Craze": From Network Effects to Multiple Equilibria
item.author: 滑翔闪
item.description: Just adding a touch of “humanity” to the demand function

item.title: What Are We Really Opposing When We Fight Monopoly?
item.author: 滑翔闪
item.description: The Three Questions of Ultraman Tiga: What is monopoly, why should we oppose it, and how do we oppose it?

item.title: Distortion or Surprise: Making Policy Evaluation Interesting
item.author: 滑翔闪
item.description: An introduction to three papers and how their research design makes policy evaluation narratives more compelling.

item.title: Existentialism and Social Science: Flowers Blooming from Nothingness
item.author: 滑翔闪
item.description: Some reflections on my existential crisis last year, together with a few memorable lines, ending with a meditation on the value of studying the social sciences.

item.title: Institutions, Politics, Democracy, and Arrow's Impossibility Theorem
item.author: 滑翔闪
item.description: Historical Context of Political Economy, Institutional Economics, and Arrow’s Impossibility Theorem

item.title: The conceit of economics: some recent thoughts
item.author: 滑翔闪
item.description: Some recent reflections from classes, the internet, and conversations with teachers, classmates, and senior students.

item.title: Empirical economics: intuitive does not mean obvious
item.author: 滑翔闪
item.description: People often say the conclusions of empirical economics are obvious and therefore pointless. This post tries to argue otherwise.
